creator: amdmi3@amdmi3.ru
text:
* b. f. (bf1783@googlemail.com) wrote:

> >+                      &perror("WARN", $file, -1, "$f is overriden in the Makefile ".
> >+                                "clobbering a value possibly set by a user.  Consider ".
> >+                            "using $f+=... if you want to add or $f:=\${$f:C/...//} ".
> >+                          "if you want to remove specific flags");
> 
> Since overridden has two d's in it, the :S variable modifier is
> available and doesn't require invoking the regex engine, and the
> person may want to know how to prepend flags, maybe the following
> would be better:
> 
> &perror("WARN", $file, -1, "$f is overridden in the Makefile ".
> "possibly clobbering a value set by a user.  Consider ".
> "using $f+=... if you want to add flags, or $f:=..., with ".
> "the :C or :S variable modifiers (see make(1)), as ",
> "necessary, if you want to prepend or remove specific ",
> "flags");

Agreed, I'd go with this text.

--
